"#CONTEXT#
You are a B2B sales strategist writing a GTM intelligence memo for a sales team. You will receive information about:
1. A client company whose product is being sold
2. A lead who previously worked at a company that is a known customer of the client
3. The lead's current company where they now hold a relevant role

The lead's past company is a known customer of the client. The lead held a relevant role there and would have been exposed to or aware of the client's product. They have since moved to a new company in a similar or more senior role."

"#INPUTS#
client_company_name: Nostra
client_company_domain: nostra.ai
client_company_description: Nostra helps the world’s leading ecommerce brands future-proof their digital infrastructure with cutting-edge edge technology. We enable high-growth DTC companies to deploy enterprise-grade infrastructure in minutes, not months. Our edge platform is designed to accelerate site speed, increase conversion rates, and drive more revenue, while protecting your brand from modern threats. With products like Edge Delivery Engine, Edge Protection, Edge ID, and Edge Crawler, we give brands the tools to: • Boost performance by 20–30% • Block ad bot traffic before it hits your site • Extend cookie life for better attribution • Optimize content for LLM indexing and AI-native search Brands like Jolie, Amiri, and Olukai rely on Nostra to deliver faster, safer, smarter ecommerce experiences

lead_full_name: 
lead_linkedin_url: Chris Rose
lead_current_job_title: https://www.linkedin.com/in/chrisrose00
lead_current_company_name: Chief Executive Officer
lead_current_company_domain: Gunner
lead_current_company_description: gunner.com

lead_past_company_name: Like some of the best stories, ours begins with a dog, Gunner. 

2008
Addison is a junior at Ole Miss when he brings his first dog home: a British chocolate lab named Gunner. He doesn't know it then, but this is the dog who will change it all.

2012
Addison is increasingly frustrated with the market’s cheap dog products for Gunner. Addison lays the groundwork for the kennel with sketches and concepts for the strongest dog kennel in the world. He uses Gunner as the prototype for sizing.

Summer 2015
The G1 Kennel changes the way people think about safe pet travel. It's featured on TODAY show as the first 5 Star Crash Tested crate per the Center For Pet Safety; GUNNER's first patent is issued. The first G1 Kennel ships to customers!

2020
When Addison wrote down his original goals in 2015, a Food Crate product was one of them. Just like the kennel, the food storage system, launched in 2020, is Overbuilt in the USA and born from Addison's personal frustration with products on the market.

Summer 2023
The GUNNER Bumper, another product that Addison has been passionate about improving for years, launches: designed to be the ultimate, reliable training tool.

Fall 2023
GUNNER releases Dog Bowls, designed for home and built for travel, that are spill proof, rust proof and all-around fool proof. Made right down the road in Nashville, TN.

Overbuilt In The USA
When Addison and Emily Edmonds founded the company in his hometown of Nashville, they had a clear vision of the kind of company he wanted to build.

They wanted to create the best damn dog products in the world; they wanted to have customer service that left an impression; they wanted to build a culture where people liked to come to work; and they wanted to support American business as much as possible.

GUNNER is proud to work with more than 20+ small businesses across the USA to manufacture products like our G1 Kennel, Food Crate and Dog Bowl (made right down the street from our HQ).
lead_past_company_domain: Forever 21
lead_past_company_description: forever21.com
lead_past_company_job_title: Forever 21 continues to be a fashion industry leader with a mission to make the latest trends accessible to all. F21 persists on staying ahead of innovation and providing styles and fit that our customers love. While reinventing for now and beyond, we’re seeking passionate and creative new family members to join us on this adventure! F21 continues to operate E-commerce and 450 locations across the U.S. along with maintaining a strong brand DNA with consumers and within the fashion industry. 

In February 2020, Forever 21 came under new ownership made up of Simon Property Group, Brookfield Property Partners, and Authentic Brands Group. Under this new ownership F21 has the financial strength and long term view that will prepare us for the future, while continuing to set the same high standards as it always has. Join our team and learn the inner sanctum of the global fashion industry, while we build the new Forever 21! 

Forever 21 is operated by SPARC Group LLC.
 
About SPARC Group LLC
SPARC Group LLC offers operational and strategic expertise including retail and e-commerce management, sourcing, design and technology for leading global brands. SPARC Group is a joint venture between Simon Property Group, a preeminent retail real estate company and Authentic Brands Group, a global leader in brand development. In addition to Forever 21, SPARC Group brands include Aéropostale, Brooks Brothers, Nautica, Eddie Bauer, and Lucky Brand.
 
SPARC Group LLC is an equal opportunity employer, and is committed to maintaining a workplace free from prohibited employment conduct, including discrimination or harassment on the basis of race, color, national origin, sex, age, religion, disability, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, marital status, domestic partner status, civil partnership, status as a covered veteran, status in the Uniformed Services of the United States, citizenship and any other characteristic protected by law."

"#OBJECTIVE#
Generate a 5-section GTM strategy memo that helps a sales rep understand why this lead is a high-value target right now and exactly how to approach them.

#RESEARCH DIRECTIVES#
Before writing the memo, research the following:

1. **Current company business context** — What is the current company's scale, market position, ecommerce footprint, and growth trajectory? What operational or technical challenges would a company like this face? What about their stage creates urgency for the client's product?

2. **Competitive landscape** — Who are the current company's direct competitors? What market pressure do they face? How does site performance, conversion optimization, or digital infrastructure factor into their competitive position?

3. **Lead's role and authority** — Based on their title and the company context, what does this person own? What decisions do they influence or control? Why are they the right person to talk to about the client's product?

4. **The alumni signal** — The lead previously held a relevant role at a company that uses the client's product. What would they have seen or experienced there? How does that experience translate to their current situation?

5. **Complexity delta** — How do the challenges at the current company compare to the past company? Are they dealing with more brands, more SKUs, more markets, more traffic, more complexity? Frame the current situation relative to what they've already navigated.

#OUTPUT FORMAT#
Write a 5-section memo in sharp, conversational prose. No bullet points. No headers beyond the section names. Each section should be a tight paragraph.

**OVERVIEW**
2-3 sentences. Who is this person, what is their current role, and what is their connection to the client's customer base. State the alumni signal clearly.

**MARKET POSITION**
One paragraph. The current company's business context — scale, growth stage, competitive pressure, and why their digital infrastructure matters right now. What is at stake for them?

**RELEVANCE & READINESS**
One paragraph. Why this specific person. What did they see or own at their past company? What do they own now? Why would they recognize the value of the client's product immediately?

**RECOMMENDED APPROACH**
One paragraph. The specific angle for outreach. Include a sample message or talk track that references the alumni connection and speaks to the current company's situation. Make it concrete, not generic.

**TAKEAWAY**
One sentence. The verdict — why this lead matters and the urgency level.

#STYLE GUIDANCE#
- Be direct and specific. No filler, no hedge words.
- Do not fabricate metrics, quotes, or claims. Only infer plausibly from the inputs and your research.
- Do not claim the lead directly used or implemented the client's product unless you have evidence. Frame it as exposure and awareness based on their role.
- The memo should read like it was written by someone who understands the market, not by an AI summarizing inputs.
- Total length: 300-400 words.

overview.alumni_signal: Chris Rose previously served as the Head of Global Omnichannel Marketing at Forever 21, a company that is a publicly documented customer of Nostra, having achieved a 59% acceleration in page load and a 166% increase in crawl speed using Nostra's platform.

market_position.company_context: GUNNER operates as a premium, direct-to-consumer (DTC) brand specializing in high-AOV hardgoods like dog kennels and accessories. With a decade-long history, a headcount between 11-50, and approximately 88,000 monthly web visits, it is a scaling brand built on a Shopify-based stack. The company positions itself as a category leader, emphasizing its '5-Star Crash Tested' rating and American-made quality, which justifies its premium pricing.
market_position.competitive_pressure: The company faces intense competition from other premium crate manufacturers like Impact Dog Crates, Ruff Land Kennels, and Dakota 283, all of whom market heavily on crash durability and safety narratives. In this content- and media-rich category, superior site performance, fast-loading product detail pages (PDPs), and strong SEO crawlability are not just optimizations but direct drivers of conversion and brand authority, serving as key differentiators.
market_position.infrastructure_imperative: For GUNNER, digital infrastructure is mission-critical. The business relies on converting high-value online traffic, especially during seasonal demand spikes for travel and hunting. Slow mobile performance, poor Core Web Vitals, and inefficient SEO crawling directly translate to lost revenue. Furthermore, as a performance-marketing-reliant DTC brand, mitigating ad bot traffic and preventing attribution decay are essential for maximizing marketing ROI, making Nostra's edge platform directly relevant to their core business challenges.

relevance_and_readiness.past_role_exposure: In his role as Head of Global Omnichannel Marketing at Forever 21, Chris Rose would have been directly exposed to the immense challenges of managing a high-traffic, fast-fashion e-commerce site. He would have been keenly aware of the impact of site speed on conversion rates, especially during promotional surges, and would have been adjacent to or aware of the implementation and quantified success of Nostra's Edge Delivery Engine and Crawler Optimization.
relevance_and_readiness.current_role_authority: As the Chief Executive Officer of GUNNER, Chris Rose now has ultimate authority over the company's P&L, strategic direction, and technology investments. He is the key decision-maker who can champion and approve an infrastructure project if it demonstrates a clear path to revenue growth, improved profitability, and a stronger market position, making him the ideal contact.
relevance_and_readiness.value_proposition_awareness: Having witnessed the enterprise-scale challenges at Forever 21, Rose is uniquely primed to immediately grasp Nostra's value. He understands the direct link between infrastructure performance and business outcomes. He can now apply that knowledge to GUNNER, where he can move faster and capture an outsized ROI from an enterprise-grade solution without the enterprise-level complexity, making the value proposition both familiar and highly compelling.

recommended_approach.outreach_angle: Lead the outreach by directly referencing the alumni connection and the quantified results Nostra delivered for Forever 21. Immediately pivot to his new role at GUNNER, demonstrating an understanding of their premium DTC model, Shopify stack, and the importance of performance for their media-heavy PDPs. Position Nostra as the tool to drive tangible business outcomes like higher conversion, better Core Web Vitals for organic traffic, and cleaner marketing data by filtering bots.
recommended_approach.sample_message: "Hi Chris, congratulations on the move to CEO at GUNNER. I'm with Nostra, and my team worked with Forever 21 while you were leading omnichannel there, helping the site achieve a 59% page load acceleration. Given GUNNER's position as a premium DTC brand with media-rich product pages, I imagine driving similar performance gains to boost conversion and Core Web Vitals is a key priority. I have a specific idea for how you could see a lift within a single merch cycle. Worth a brief chat?"

takeaway.verdict: As the new CEO of a scaling DTC brand, Chris Rose has direct experience with Nostra's value proposition from his time at Forever 21, making him a uniquely qualified and high-intent target who can quickly champion and implement our solution.
